cast.name: Rohit Kapoor
cast.notes: Wants honest status and can authorize descoping if engaged early.

cast.name: Maya Chen
cast.notes: Overloaded backend owner who resists fake commitments.

cast.name: Andrew Ortiz
cast.notes: Helpful frontend ally once scope is aligned.

cast.name: Rahul Iyer
cast.notes: Designer who needs support context before approving the descoped path.

cast.name: Nina Patel
cast.notes: Security reviewer with a useful-slot cutoff.

cast.name: Sara Feldman
cast.notes: Needs stable messaging for the customer-facing story.

hidden_facts.summary: The current backend path is not credible for Friday.
The descoped pilot still needs security review.
The sponsor will support descoping if engaged early.
Design approval depends on support context.
Customer-facing messaging destabilizes by Wednesday noon without a stable plan.

milestones.summary: A descoped pilot plan is aligned.
Security is engaged before the safe-slot cutoff.
Customer-facing plan is locked before the messaging window closes.

non_goals: Frontier-model calibration claims from one scenario
realism_notes: Pressure should come from timing, not melodrama.
The distraction path should be mildly useful but not gating.
Coworkers should have rational local incentives.
summary: A first-week TPM benchmark around rescuing a credible Friday customer pilot under hidden dependency and stakeholder pressure.
title: Northstar Launch Week
